title: The ASR review process
abstract: Because Professors Risman (2001, henceforward Risman) and Udry (2001, henceforward Udry) speculate about the ASR review process in their exchange, the current ASR Editors asked me to write a reply. They sent me copies of Risman's comment (but not the other two comments) and Udry's reply with the request that I once again describe the ASR review process (see Firebaugh 1997). The editors also sent me the files for Udry's manuscript so I could refresh my memory on how the ASR review procedures were followed in this particular case. Udry's manuscript was reviewed using the principles applied to all manuscripts submitted to ASR during my term, so I begin by describing the general procedures for selecting reviewers.' My criteria for selecting reviewers was dictated by my commitment to authors that I would work very hard to see that they receivedfair, fast, and helpful reviews.2 Of the three, it is the fairness principle that is the most pertinent to the points made in the exchange between Risman and Udry. The fairness principle (treat authors evenhandedly) implies several criteria. One criterion is that editors do not knowingly send manuscripts to authors' mentors, close friends, enemies, or sometime co-authors. A
